name: lstcam-calib

metadata.name: lstcam-calib
metadata.summary: Camera calibration code for the LSTCam

documentation:
# lstcam_calib

Repository for low-level camera calibration tools for the LST Camera.

modules.name: lstcam_calib.tools.create_cat_b_calibration_file
documentation:
Tool to compute Cat-B camera calibration coefficients (dc_to_pe and pedestals).

The estimation is performed with the F-factor method and
based on interleaved flat-field and pedestal events.

The estimated coefficients are relative to the Cat-A calibration (i.e. in the
case of identical conditions : dc_to_pe = 1 and pedestals = 0)

```python
lstcam_calib_create_cat_b_calibration_file --help
```

documentation: Calibration coefficient calculator.

documentation: Do final actions.
Tool that generates a HDF5 file with Cat-B camera calibration coefficients.

It computes the F-factor systematics corrections (quadratic noise term).


This is useful to estimate the quadratic noise term to include in the
standard F-factor formula.

functions.name: lstcam_calib.pixel.utils.check_outlier_mask
documentation:
Check a outlier mask for too many True values.

Parameters
----------
outliers : np.ndarray
    Array of shape (n_events, n_pixels) and dtype bool.
log : logging.Logger
    Warnings are given to this logger
kind : str
    Should be "pedestal" or "flatfield"
n_pixels_threshold : int
    A warning is raised if more than this number of pixels are outliers

methods.name: lstcam_calib.pixel.calibration_calculator.LSTCalibrationCalculator.calculate_calibration_coefficients
documentation:
Calculate calibration coefficients from flatfield and pedestal statistics.

Parameters
----------
event: ArrayArrayEventContainer

methods.name: lstcam_calib.pixel.calibration_calculator.LSTCalibrationCalculator.expected_npe_std
documentation:
Estimate the expected standard deviation of the estimated npe over the camera.

This is given in principle by:

std_pe_mean=std_npe/sqrt((n_events)+ (relative_qe_dispersion*npe)**2)

where the relative_qe_dispersion is mainly due to different detection QE among PMs.

However, due to the systematics correction associated to the B term, a linear and quadratic
noise component  must be added, these components depend on the sample statistics per pixel (n_events).
The parameters in this function (linear_noise_params and quadratic_noise_params) have been obtained with
a fit of the std of filter scan taken in date 2023/05/10 and considering
n_events = [1000,2500,5000,7500,10000,30000]

Calibration calculator for LST camera.

Fills the MonitoringCameraContainer on the base of calibration events

methods.name: lstcam_calib.pixel.flatfield.FlasherFlatFieldCalculator.calculate_relative_gain_results
documentation: Calculate and return the sample statistics.

Calculates flat-field parameters from flasher data.

Based on the best algorithm described by S. Fegan in MST-CAM-TN-0060 (eq. 19)
Pixels are defined as outliers on the base of a cut on the pixel charge median
over the full sample distribution and the pixel signal time inside the
waveform time.

methods.name: lstcam_calib.tools.create_drs4_pedestal_file.DRS4PedestalAndSpikeHeight.check_baseline_values
documentation:
Check the values of the drs4 baseline for issues.

In case of no EVBv5 data or no pre-calibration by EVBv6,
we expect no negative and no small values.

In case of EVBv6 applied baseline corrections, values should
be close to 0, but negative values are ok.

methods.name: lstcam_calib.pixel.pedestals.PedestalIntegrator.calculate_pedestal_results
documentation: Calculate and return the sample statistics.

Calculates pedestal parameters integrating the charge of pedestal events.

The pedestal value corresponds to the charge estimated with the selected
charge extractor
The pixels are set as outliers on the base of a cut on the pixel charge median
over the pedestal sample and the pixel charge standard deviation over
the pedestal sample with respect to the camera median values.


Parameters
----------
charge_median_cut_outliers : List[2]
    Interval (number of std) of accepted charge values around camera median value
charge_std_cut_outliers : List[2]
    Interval (number of std) of accepted charge standard deviation around camera median value

methods.name: lstcam_calib.drs4.time_correction_calculator.TimeCorrectionCalculator.calib_peak_time_jit
documentation:
Numba function for calibration pulse time.

Parameters
----------
pulse : ndarray
    Pulse time stored in a numpy array of shape
    (n_gain, n_pixels).
charge : ndarray
    Charge in each pixel.
    (n_gain, n_pixels).
first_cap_array : ndarray
    Value of first capacitor stored in a numpy array of shape
    (n_clus, n_gain, n_pix).
mean_values_per_bin : ndarray
    Array to fill using pulse time
    stored in a numpy array of shape
    (n_gain, n_pixels, n_bins).
entries_per_bin : ndarray
    Array to store number of entries per bin
    stored in a numpy array of shape
    (n_gain, n_pixels, n_bins).
n_cap : int
    Number of capacitors
n_combine : int
    Number of combine capacitors in a single bin

gain: int

n harmonic

bn: ndarray

Create h5 file with time correction values of DRS4.

Description of this method: "Analysis techniques
and performance of the Domino Ring Sampler version 4
based readout for the MAGIC telescopes [arxiv:1305.1007]
